Full job description
About the Role

We’re looking for an experienced and driven Business Delivery Manager to lead the operational delivery of our Integrated Urgent Care (IUC) services.

This is a senior leadership role where you’ll take ownership of multiple service contracts, ensuring high-quality patient care, strong operational performance, and delivery against key contractual and financial targets.

You’ll work closely with senior clinical and operational leaders, acting as the key link between frontline services and executive leadership—while building trusted relationships with external stakeholders, including NHS commissioners and partners.

As a senior leader within a 24/7 service, you will be expected to work and maintain a visible presence on a regular basis during out-of-hours periods.

Key Responsibilities

Lead all IUC contracts within the portfolio, ensuring services meet customer requirements and contractual KPIs.
Provide proactive contract and stakeholder management, escalating risks and reporting performance as appropriate.
Work in partnership with clinical and medical leads to deliver high standards of performance and behaviour.
Ensure effective workforce planning, rota fulfilment, and recruitment to meet service demand and financial targets.
Use accurate management information and data to drive performance and decision-making.
Maintain governance and ensure contracts are compliant and aligned with regulatory standards.
Foster a collaborative, high-performance culture, supporting accountability and engagement across teams.
Lead budgeting, forecasting, and workforce planning processes.
Support and manage direct reports, including participation in on-call and deputising responsibilities.
Deliver financial and operational performance targets, including efficiency and cost improvement initiatives.
Lead change programmes, contract mobilisation, and business continuity planning.
Build and maintain effective stakeholder relationships and represent services internally and externally.
Promote continuous improvement, innovation, and best practice sharing.
Ensure patient safety through effective governance, risk management, and learning processes.
Lead people management activities including performance, development, and succession planning.
Ensure compliance with regulatory, health and safety, and organisational requirements
About You

You’ll be a confident, resilient leader who can operate in a complex, fast-paced healthcare environment.

Experience & Knowledge

Essential

Significant senior leadership experience in a complex organisation
Proven track record in operational and contract management within multi-stakeholder environments
Experience leading teams through change and transformation
Experience working with external partners and stakeholders
Ability to manage performance, budgets, and workforce planning
Strategic thinker with strong decision-making capability
Understanding of governance and regulatory compliance
Desirable

Experience within healthcare or urgent care services
Knowledge of NHS contracts (e.g. APMS, GMS, NHS Standard Contract)
Experience managing large-scale services or business units (£5m+ turnover)
Background in service mobilisation or multi-site operations
Skills & Abilities

Demonstrates integrity and accountability in all interactions
Provides values-led leadership and supports high standards of performance
Ability to lead, motivate, and develop teams while fostering collaboration and inclusion
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Resilient, with the ability to operate effectively in complex operational environments
Effective organisational and workload management skills
IT literate
Why Join Us?

At PHL, we pride ourselves on being Truly Caring, Team First, and Pioneering Innovation. You’ll join a forward-thinking organisation that values integrity, inclusivity, and innovation, and empowers its leaders to make a real difference.

Applications

Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. If your application is successful, you can expect to hear from us within 2 weeks of submission.

Please note that this advert will remain open until the position is filled. Interviews will be held throughout the advertising period, so we encourage you to apply early to avoid disappointment.

personalisedIntro: I’ve spent my career stepping into complex, service-critical environments where performance, governance, and day-to-day delivery all matter at once. I’m comfortable in roles where I need to balance operational detail with senior stakeholder management and steady decision-making.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can hold a complex service together without losing sight of the basics: performance, staffing, compliance, and contract delivery. It also needs someone who can work credibly with clinical leaders, external partners, and frontline teams while keeping a calm grip on risk and change.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Senior leadership in a complex, fast-paced environment
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations through scale-up in a specialist manufacturing setting, where delivery discipline and resilience mattered every day.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can operate at pace and keep control when the environment is changing.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: In NHS primary care, I built stronger reporting around workflow, room use, payroll, and contractual income, and used that to improve performance.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can work with live service data and turn it into better control.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve coordinated workload allocation and training across a 12-person function, which required clear prioritisation and steady oversight.
experienceMappings.relevance: Useful evidence for keeping staffing and service standards aligned.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a tracking system to monitor ride operating hours and support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical operation.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I’m comfortable building practical controls where compliance matters.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Stakeholder management across internal and external groups

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation and released £33.3k of working capital through debtor reduction.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can keep an eye on financial outcomes as part of operational delivery.

focusAreasToBring.title: Contract and performance grip
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making performance visible, understanding where contracts are under pressure, and keeping escalation and reporting disciplined.

focusAreasToBring.title: Workforce and service resilience
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would pay close attention to rota fulfilment, recruitment, and the practical steps needed to keep a 24/7 service stable.

focusAreasToBring.title: Governance and improvement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would look for simple, workable controls that protect compliance, support patient safety, and make improvement easier to sustain.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring senior operational experience across healthcare, regulated services, and complex service environments, with a track record of improving performance without losing control of the basics. I’ve worked with data, governance, workforce, and financial levers in roles where delivery had to be consistent and visible. That makes me a strong match for a role that needs both operational calm and practical leadership.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months tightening visibility across contracts, performance, and workforce demand. I’d also focus on building strong working relationships with clinical leaders and external stakeholders, while getting a clear handle on the controls that protect patient safety, compliance, and financial delivery. From there, I’d look for practical improvements in reporting, rota resilience, and day-to-day operational consistency.
companyHighlights: Partnering Health Ltd delivers Integrated Urgent Care services.
The organisation describes itself as Truly Caring, Team First, Pioneering Innovation, with integrity and inclusivity as clear values.
The role sits in a 24/7 service with visible leadership expectations, including out-of-hours presence.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people expect accountability, clear communication, and steady follow-through. The values and collaborative tone in Partnering Health Ltd’s brief feel aligned with how I lead: practical, direct, and focused on what helps the service work better.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the contracts, performance data, workforce position, governance rhythm, and key stakeholders so I can see where pressure sits and what good looks like in practice.
I would work with clinical, operational, and external partners to tighten reporting, clarify priorities, and address any immediate risks around staffing, compliance, or performance.
I would look for the most practical efficiency and service improvements, then start embedding clearer routines for forecasting, escalation, and continuous improvement.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring a steady, hands-on approach to helping Partnering Health Ltd keep Integrated Urgent Care services safe, compliant, and performing well.
closingProofPoints: I improved patient experience in live NHS primary care, with reviews rising from 2.09 to 4.51.
I supported £149.9k profit improvement and released £33.3k of working capital in a healthcare setting.
I’ve led operations through scale-up, compliance control, and team coordination in complex environments.
contentNotes: I have used the strongest available healthcare evidence from NHS primary care rather than inventing urgent care-specific experience.
I have kept companyHighlights limited to facts directly stated in the advert.
The evidence bank is thinner on direct NHS contract examples, so I have framed contract fit through transferable healthcare, reporting, and governance experience.
